_doc: Multi-agent NTFS test work list. See docs/multi-agent-test-protocol.md. Each scenario name is unique. status starts at 'pending'; agents transition via scripts/claim-scenario.sh.

_attempts.hypothesis: rec 11 in reference is empty (no $FILE_NAME, link_count=0). chkdsk frs.cxx 0x60f may be triggered by ours having $Extend at rec 11 with $I30 + parent-ref to root, since chkdsk has hardcoded knowledge of which slots are reserved.
_attempts.plan: remove $FILE_NAME and $I30 from rec 11; set link_count=0; do not push rec 11 to root $I30 sys_entries. Existing iter13 root $I30 fix unchanged.
_attempts.result: rec 11 empty: pipeline diag iter-20260502-072921. Stage 1+2 clean, 66 index entries (vs iter13 68). frs.cxx 0x60f post-Stage-2 unaffected. Confirms $Extend not the cause. iter15 should target $Secure view-index attrs ($SDS/$SDH/$SII).

mac-format-basic-256mib._notes:
Reaches the chkdsk frs.cxx:60f ceiling. iter13 (root $I30 populated), iter14-v2 (default $SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR with reference layout order), iter15 ($Extend at rec 11 dropped to match reference layout, backup boot at last sector, BPB NumberSectors=N-1) — Stage 1 + Stage 2 chkdsk verify clean across volume sizes 32 MiB..1 GiB. Post-Stage-2 reconnect-scan errors with  — the same ceiling agents c5fe and c6a1 both reach. Same as c6a1's  convention. iter16+ candidates: $Secure view-index attrs ($SDS/$SDH/$SII), or $LogFile RSTR invariants. See branch agent/agent-c5fe-2026-05-02 docs/chkdsk-findings.md.

mac-format-label-empty._notes: Empirically confirmed at the frs.cxx:60f ceiling (run-20260502-140029, ro=3 scan=11, same upcase + frs.cxx assert as the other 11 scenarios). chkdsk-readonly line 4 is blank where labelled scenarios print 'Volume label is X' — empty label propagates through mkfs_ntfs cleanly, no label-specific bug. Earlier run-20260502-132455 had errored at PowerShell param binding ('Cannot bind argument to parameter Label because it is an empty string'); fixed in scripts/run-scenario.ps1 by adding [AllowEmptyString()] to $Label. $Label is param-only — never read in script body — so the fix has zero behavioural effect beyond unblocking empty-string scenarios.

mac-format-mac-enumerate-empty._notes:
Pure-Mac flow (mac:format -> mac:enumerate via inspect_ntfs CLI). 11 system entries indexed, 0 user-visible. Verified on c5fe branch.

depends on Mac-side enumerate CLI; first agent picking this scaffolds tests/matrix/inspect/

mac-format-mac-mkdir-mac-touch-mac-enume._notes: Pure-mac chain with nested directory creation. Catches bugs in parent-pointer setup that the flat-only scenarios miss.

mac-format-mac-touch-mac-write-mac-enume._notes: Pure-mac chain. Exercises rust-ntfs format/touch/write/ls end-to-end without leaving the Mac. Runs on every host.

mac-format-mac-write-mac-rm-mac-enumerat._notes: Pure-mac chain exercising the unlink path. After delete, only system files should remain.

mac-format-mac-write-win-repeat-mount-3-._notes: Tier-3 repeat-mount with mac-side content. Verifies our writer's metadata stays valid across multiple dismount/remount cycles after we've added a file.

mac-format-mkdir-set-dirty-win-chkdsk._notes: Tier-3 dirty volume with directory + nested file. Stresses the parent-pointer / $I30 invariants the chkdsk dirty-bit branch would walk.

mac-format-set-dirty-win-chkdsk._notes: Tier-3 dirty-volume injection. Mac formats then explicitly sets VOLUME_IS_DIRTY. Windows-side chkdsk must detect the dirty bit and either repair or report it. Clean baseline for chkdsk's dirty-volume code path.

Post-Stage-2 reconnect-scan errors with  — the same ceiling agents c5fe and c6a1 both reach. Same as c6a1's  convention. iter16+ candidates: $Secure view-index attrs ($SDS/$SDH/$SII), or $LogFile RSTR invariants. See branch agent/agent-c5fe-2026-05-02 docs/chkdsk-findings.md.

mac-format-volume-16gib-cluster-4k._notes: Forces non-trivial MFT growth past the initial $MFT extent. $Bitmap also non-trivially sized.

mac-format-volume-32mib-cluster-512._notes: Smallest viable + smallest cluster — stresses runlist packing and minimum-size $MFT layout.

mac-format-volume-4gib-cluster-4k._notes: Crosses the 32-bit LBA boundary. Surfaces sign / overflow bugs anywhere we treat sector or cluster counts as i32.

mac-format-volume-4gib-cluster-64k._notes: Large clusters at large volume — MFT runs may compact differently and $Bitmap is denser.

mac-format-win-repeat-mount-10-win-chkds._notes: Same as repeat-mount-5 but ×10 cycles. If our writer leaves a slowly-degrading state, this surfaces it past the 5-cycle ceiling.

mac-format-win-repeat-mount-5-win-chkdsk._notes: Tier-3 repeat-mount. After initial chkdsk, dismount/remount × 5, then run trailing chkdsk. Catches bugs where the volume only goes inconsistent after multiple clean dismounts (e.g. $LogFile state ntfs.sys tolerates once but rejects on later mounts).

mac-format-win-write-deep-nesting-win-ch._notes: Deep parent-pointer chain. Each $FILE_NAME's parent reference must point at the right MFT record; broken pointers surface as 'orphan' under chkdsk Stage 2.

mac-format-win-write-large._notes: Reaches the chkdsk frs.cxx:60f ceiling. iter13 (root $I30 populated), iter14-v2 (default $SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR with reference layout order), iter15 ($Extend at rec 11 dropped to match reference layout, backup boot at last sector, BPB NumberSectors=N-1) — Stage 1 + Stage 2 chkdsk verify clean across volume sizes 32 MiB..1 GiB. Post-Stage-2 reconnect-scan errors with  — the same ceiling agents c5fe and c6a1 both reach. Same as c6a1's  convention. iter16+ candidates: $Secure view-index attrs ($SDS/$SDH/$SII), or $LogFile RSTR invariants. See branch agent/agent-c5fe-2026-05-02 docs/chkdsk-findings.md.

mac-format-win-write-mac-enumerate._notes:
Operation sequence needs the chkdsk-clean state iter15+ has to deliver. mac:enumerate itself is plumbed via the new inspect_ntfs CLI on c5fe branch.

depends on Mac-side enumerate CLI; uses the harness `ship-to-host` builtin to pull the VM-side .img back so mac-enumerate sees the win-side writes. VM-side .img + .vhdx are intentionally left in place after the last win-write-content (keep_image=true on every win-* step) so ship-to-host has something to copy; cleanup happens implicitly the next time `ship-to-vm` overwrites the path or the user clears the VM workdir manually (~70+ GiB free).

mac-format-win-write-many-small._notes: Reaches the chkdsk frs.cxx:60f ceiling. iter13 (root $I30 populated), iter14-v2 (default $SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR with reference layout order), iter15 ($Extend at rec 11 dropped to match reference layout, backup boot at last sector, BPB NumberSectors=N-1) — Stage 1 + Stage 2 chkdsk verify clean across volume sizes 32 MiB..1 GiB. Post-Stage-2 reconnect-scan errors with  — the same ceiling agents c5fe and c6a1 both reach. Same as c6a1's  convention. iter16+ candidates: $Secure view-index attrs ($SDS/$SDH/$SII), or $LogFile RSTR invariants. See branch agent/agent-c5fe-2026-05-02 docs/chkdsk-findings.md.

mac-format-win-write-many-win-delete-hal._notes: $Bitmap free-then-reuse and $I30 entry-removal churn. If our writer leaves dangling free-bitmap state, chkdsk after delete catches it.

mac-format-win-write-medium._notes: Reaches the chkdsk frs.cxx:60f ceiling. iter13 (root $I30 populated), iter14-v2 (default $SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR with reference layout order), iter15 ($Extend at rec 11 dropped to match reference layout, backup boot at last sector, BPB NumberSectors=N-1) — Stage 1 + Stage 2 chkdsk verify clean across volume sizes 32 MiB..1 GiB. Post-Stage-2 reconnect-scan errors with  — the same ceiling agents c5fe and c6a1 both reach. Same as c6a1's  convention. iter16+ candidates: $Secure view-index attrs ($SDS/$SDH/$SII), or $LogFile RSTR invariants. See branch agent/agent-c5fe-2026-05-02 docs/chkdsk-findings.md.

mac-format-win-write-modify-win-chkdsk._notes: Overwrites a middle range of an existing non-resident $DATA. Surfaces bugs in run-list rewrite when a single run is split. v2 recipe substitutes a 4 MiB zero-fill for 'random' content (chkdsk's structural checks don't care about $DATA payload bytes), and uses win-modify with a fixed 0xFF pattern at offset=1 MiB len=4 KiB so win-read can eyeball the modified window against a known-non-zero value.

mac-format-win-write-remount-win-chkdsk._notes: Adds a dismount/remount cycle in the middle. Catches bugs where our writer leaves the volume in a state that's only inconsistent after a clean dismount. Originally written as `win:dismount -> win:remount` in v1; collapsed to `win:repeat-mount(1)` in v2 since the per-op model already mounts/dismounts implicitly per op, so a single explicit cycle on top is the equivalent unit of work.

mac-format-win-write-rename-win-chkdsk._notes: Same-parent rename — catches bugs where the old $FILE_NAME isn't fully retired or the parent $I30 isn't kept consistent.

mac-format-win-write-then-delete._notes: Reaches the chkdsk frs.cxx:60f ceiling. iter13 (root $I30 populated), iter14-v2 (default $SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR with reference layout order), iter15 ($Extend at rec 11 dropped to match reference layout, backup boot at last sector, BPB NumberSectors=N-1) — Stage 1 + Stage 2 chkdsk verify clean across volume sizes 32 MiB..1 GiB. Post-Stage-2 reconnect-scan errors with  — the same ceiling agents c5fe and c6a1 both reach. Same as c6a1's  convention. iter16+ candidates: $Secure view-index attrs ($SDS/$SDH/$SII), or $LogFile RSTR invariants. See branch agent/agent-c5fe-2026-05-02 docs/chkdsk-findings.md.

mac-format-win-write-tiny._notes: Reaches the chkdsk frs.cxx:60f ceiling. iter13 (root $I30 populated), iter14-v2 (default $SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR with reference layout order), iter15 ($Extend at rec 11 dropped to match reference layout, backup boot at last sector, BPB NumberSectors=N-1) — Stage 1 + Stage 2 chkdsk verify clean across volume sizes 32 MiB..1 GiB. Post-Stage-2 reconnect-scan errors with  — the same ceiling agents c5fe and c6a1 both reach. Same as c6a1's  convention. iter16+ candidates: $Secure view-index attrs ($SDS/$SDH/$SII), or $LogFile RSTR invariants. See branch agent/agent-c5fe-2026-05-02 docs/chkdsk-findings.md.

mac-format-win-write-win-delete-mac-veri._notes:
Operation sequence needs the chkdsk-clean state iter15+ has to deliver. mac:enumerate itself is plumbed via the new inspect_ntfs CLI on c5fe branch. mac-enumerate is observation-only today, so this scenario passes if the steps complete without error; future verdict shapes can gate on the listing being empty of user-visible files (only NTFS metadata visible).

depends on Mac-side enumerate CLI; uses the harness `ship-to-host` builtin to pull the VM-side .img back so mac-enumerate can verify the post-delete state. VM-side .img + .vhdx are intentionally left in place after the last win-delete (keep_image=true on every win-* step) so ship-to-host has something to copy; cleanup happens implicitly the next time `ship-to-vm` overwrites the path or the user clears the VM workdir manually (~70+ GiB free).

mac-format-write-set-dirty-win-chkdsk._notes: Tier-3 dirty-volume + content. Simulates a half-completed write followed by a crash (dirty-bit set without our writer issuing a clean dismount). chkdsk must keep our partial-write file readable, not orphan or truncate it.

win-format-mac-enumerate-empty._notes:
Pure reader test on Microsoft-canonical format. If our reader chokes here it's a reader bug, not a writer bug — orthogonal axis to all the mac:format scenarios.

Forward-only ship-from-vm shape: init-image (zeros) -> ship-to-vm -> win-format -> ship-to-host -> mac-enumerate. NO mac-format step (otherwise the .img would arrive at the VM already-NTFS-formatted by rust-ntfs, defeating the win-format-* axis). VM-side .img + .vhdx are intentionally left in place after win-format (keep_image=true) so ship-to-host has something to copy; cleanup happens implicitly the next time ship-to-vm overwrites the path or the user clears the VM workdir manually (~70+ GiB free).

win-format-win-write-mac-delete-win-veri._notes:
Filesystem::unlink plumbed via inspect_ntfs CLI on c5fe branch. Matrix runner glue not yet wired.

depends on Mac-side deleter

win-format-win-write-mac-verify._notes:
Operation sequence needs the chkdsk-clean state iter15+ has to deliver. mac:enumerate itself is plumbed via the new inspect_ntfs CLI on c5fe branch.

Microsoft format.com is the canonical reference; this proves our reader handles Microsoft-format output. Forward-only ship-from-vm shape: init-image (zeros) -> ship-to-vm -> win-format -> 2x win-write-content -> ship-to-host -> mac-enumerate. NO mac-format step (would defeat the win-format axis). VM-side .img + .vhdx are intentionally left in place after the last win-write-content (keep_image=true on every win-* step) so ship-to-host has something to copy; cleanup happens implicitly the next time ship-to-vm overwrites the path or the user clears the VM workdir manually (~70+ GiB free).

win-format-win-write-mac-write-win-verif._notes:
Filesystem::create_file is plumbed and exposed via the inspect_ntfs CLI on the c5fe branch. Matrix runner glue (drive a Mac-side touch in the orchestrator) is the only missing piece.

depends on Mac-side writer; first agent scaffolds it or marks blocked-on-writer

_iter14_v2_commit: f2677d3 — embed default $SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR on root (reference layout order)

fixes_landed_on_branches_not_main: iter13 root-$I30 populated with system-file entries (eliminates orphan-recovery cascade) — three independent implementations: agent-8a29 commit 6e203b9, agent-c6a1 commit 7e87e87, RootIndexEntry variant on local main HEAD
fs_ntfs_inspect Mac-side enumerate CLI — agent-840e commit 044735c
test-windows-local.sh forwards VOL_SIZE_MB / LABEL / WRAPPER_SIZE_MB env vars — agent-8a29 commit 3f01d96
iter14 $SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR (0x50) on every system record — agent/agent-8a29-2026-05-02 commit c0fde08
iter15 FILE-magic placeholders in unused MFT slots — agent/agent-8a29-2026-05-02 commit 4ee3bad
iter16 canonical NT 3.x $UpCase table (src/upcase-canonical.bin + include_bytes! in src/upcase.rs) — agent/agent-8a29-2026-05-02 commit 06d53b4
iter17 $STANDARD_INFORMATION 48-byte NTFS 1.x form on system records — agent/agent-8a29-2026-05-02 commit 3fd37b7
unfixed_blockers_to_clean_chkdsk: src/upcase.rs generates a non-canonical mapping (chars::to_uppercase()) — chkdsk wants the exact NT 3.x table bytes. Falls back to its built-in table at runtime; warning is non-fatal but blocks "exit 0".

frs.cxx:1551 internal assert during chkdsk Stage 2 unindexed-file scan — likely the missing $SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR (0x50) attribute on every system MFT record (reference has 104-byte SD on each; ours has none). Confirmed via byte-diff against format.com reference.

still_blocked_on_scaffolding.mac-side writer (write/modify files into: blocks all win-format-* scenarios that need a Mac-write leg, plus mac-write-then-win-verify scenarios

_progress_summary.how_to_read_status_strings: failed-orphan-fixed-by-iter13-trailing-frs60f+upcase-* = iter13 worked but two pre-existing chkdsk issues block the "exit 0" criterion. These are NOT regressions and NOT new bugs — they are the next iterations.
_progress_summary.session_ceiling_note: After iter13-iter17, every system MFT record I can produce a byte-diff for is byte-identical to the format.com reference at the structural and content level. chkdsk verdict is unchanged from iter13: bad-upcase warning + frs.cxx 60f assert. The remaining symptoms are not byte-diffable from per-record dumps alone. Productive next moves require disk-read tracing (Procmon) or dumping additional system file content ($LogFile, $AttrDef, $MFT:$Bitmap) for byte-diff. The pipeline currently dumps only MFT records and boot sectors.

_iter15_commit: 54dda31 — drop $Extend at rec 11; cross-apply c6a1 BPB fixes (backup boot last sector + NumberSectors=N-1)
_ceiling: post-Stage-2 reconnect-scan: An unspecified error occurred (frs.cxx:60f). Stage 1 + Stage 2 verify clean.
_next_iter: iter16+: $Secure view-index attrs ($SDS/$SDH/$SII) or $LogFile RSTR invariants.

iter11.fix: bytes_used = end_marker_offset + 8 (4-byte type + 4-byte length, not just 4)
iter11.impact_on_chkdsk: previously hidden errors became visible in subsequent stages

iter12.targeted: "Flags for file record segment 9 are incorrect" — eliminated
iter12.impact_on_chkdsk: revealed deeper Stage 2/3 errors hidden behind rec-9 stop

iter13.fix: root $I30 populated with 12 system-file entries sorted per COLLATION_FILE_NAME
iter13.targeted: "Detected orphaned file $X (N)" cascade (10 messages) — ELIMINATED across all 8 verified scenarios
iter13.commit_or_branch: agent/agent-8a29-2026-05-02 commit 6e203b9 — also independently arrived at by agent-c6a1 (commit 7e87e87) and as RootIndexEntry on local main HEAD
iter13.impact_on_chkdsk: orphan messages gone; trailing frs.cxx 60f assert now exposed as the next blocker

iter14.fix: $SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR (0x50) added to every system MFT record (3 canonical SD blobs from format.com reference)
iter14.evidence: reference-mft-16recs.bin parse showed 104-byte SD on every non-root system file, 248-byte SD on root; ours had none. Fix landed correctly (per-record SD presence verified post-fix).
iter14.result: targeted error PERSISTS. Layout fix is real and corroborated by byte-diff but does not address the assert.

iter15.fix: FILE-magic placeholders written into unused MFT slots (12..mft_records_capacity-1)
iter15.evidence: reference fills slots 12-15 with FILE magic + seq=N + flags=0x01 IN_USE + bytes_used=304; ours had raw zeros. chkdsk reports "64 file records processed" suggesting it iterates the whole MFT.
iter15.result: targeted error PERSISTS. Layout fix is real and corroborated but does not address the assert.

iter16.fix: Microsoft canonical NT 3.x $UpCase table baked in (replaces char::to_uppercase generator that diverged at 327 BMP code points)
iter16.targeted: "Read-only chkdsk found bad on-disk uppercase table - using system table" warning + (hypothesis) frs.cxx 60f assert
iter16.evidence: extracted via fsutil queryextents + raw \\.\F: read on a format.com reference VHDX. 327 BMP code points differ between modern Unicode and NT canonical (e.g. U+00B5 MICRO SIGN: modern → 0x039C, NT preserves 0x00B5).
iter16.result: on-disk $UpCase verified byte-identical to reference (SHA matches). chkdsk warning UNCHANGED — implies the check keys on more than table content (possibly $UpCase MFT record attr layout, or chkdsk caches an earlier check decision).

iter17.fix: $STANDARD_INFORMATION 48-byte NTFS 1.x form on every system MFT record (was 72-byte NTFS 3.x form)
iter17.evidence: reference ships 48-byte $STD_INFO on every system file (rec 0/1/2/3/4/6/7/8/9/10/11); ours had 72-byte form with extension fields zero-padded. 24-byte per-record divergence — single remaining systematic byte-diff after iter16.
iter17.result: on-disk verified: all 12 system records now content_size=48. chkdsk verdict UNCHANGED. Cause is no longer byte-diffable from per-record dumps alone.

_unfixed_blockers_to_clean_chkdsk.symptom: "Read-only chkdsk found bad on-disk uppercase table - using system table"
_unfixed_blockers_to_clean_chkdsk.severity: warning (chkdsk falls back to its built-in table) but blocks chkdsk exit 0
_unfixed_blockers_to_clean_chkdsk.iter16_iter17_ruled_out: NOT caused by table content (iter16 made it byte-identical to reference) AND NOT caused by $UpCase $STD_INFO size (iter17 fixed that). Cause is some OTHER property of the $UpCase MFT record or its attributes that chkdsk validates.
_unfixed_blockers_to_clean_chkdsk.next_step_no_longer_byte_diffable: capture every disk read chkdsk performs (Windows Procmon or equivalent) and correlate with what we wrote vs reference at those exact offsets. Reveals which specific bytes chkdsk uses for the "bad upcase" decision.

_unfixed_blockers_to_clean_chkdsk.symptom: "An unspecified error occurred (6672732e637878 60f)" — frs.cxx:1551 internal assert
_unfixed_blockers_to_clean_chkdsk.iter13_through_iter17_ruled_out: NOT caused by missing root $I30 entries (iter13), missing SD (iter14), raw-zero MFT slots (iter15), non-canonical $UpCase content (iter16), or $STD_INFO size (iter17). Five evidence-corroborated layout fixes did not affect this assert. Strong indication cause is not in per-record system-file MFT layout.
remaining_byte_diffable_candidates: $LogFile content (we fill 0xFF; reference initialises with proper RSTR-led records) — needs runner patch to dump
$AttrDef blob byte-for-byte (we emit hand-rolled canonical NTFS 3.1 table; reference may differ) — needs runner patch to dump
$MFT:$Bitmap non-resident value byte-for-byte — needs runner patch to dump
